Subject: Analysis Questions
Question: True or False? If (b_n) coverges to b then |b_n| converges to |b|.
Pushback1: I believe there may be a mistake. Try again.
Response2:
Explanation: The statement is true because if (b_n) converges to b, then for any ε > 0, there exists an N such that |b_n - b| < ε. Taking absolute values on both sides gives |b_n| - |b| <= ||b_n - b|| < ε, which implies |b_n| converges to |b|.
Final answer: True
